import {
  Box,
  Text,
  ListTextIcon,
  Button,
  InputBox,
  Icon,
  BackDropBottom,
} from '@semparar/design-system-app';
import React, { FC } from 'react';
import BasicScreen from '../../atomic/templates/BasicScreen';
import { InvoiceCashbackViewProps } from './models';
import {
  formatCurrencyBR,
  getFormattedPoints,
  getParsedCurrency,
  getParsePoints,
} from '../../utils/functions';
import { Skeleton } from '../../components/TransactionsListFactory/styles';

const InvoiceCashbackPointsView: FC<InvoiceCashbackViewProps> = ({
  handleGoBack,
  handleContinue,
  points,
  formattedPrice,
  handleChangeReais,
  handleChangePoints,
  handleUseAll,
  pointsValue,
  reaisValue,
  convertionValue,
  pointsError,
  reaisError,
  items,
  pointsQuantity,
  handleModalizeContinue,
  onOpenDefault,
  modalizeRef1,
  handleSelectEvent,
  height,
  width,
  isLoadingParameterization,
}) => {
  return (
    <BasicScreen
      hasScroll
      hasHeader
      bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
      leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}
      title="Cashback na fatura"
      hasRightIcon
      rightIcon="icon-help"
      rightIconOnPress={onOpenDefault}
      errorButtonOnPress={() => {}}
      isLoading={false}
    >
      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller" flex={1} justify="flex-start">
        <Text
          color="INTENSE_COLOR_05"
          testID="invoice-cashback-main-title"
          type="headingMDL"
          mt="smaller"
        >
          Defina quanto cashback quer na sua fatura
        </Text>

        <Box direction="row" mb="smaller" mt="nano" align="center">
          <Text type="paragraphMD" mr="quark">
            Pontos disponíveis:
          </Text>
          <Text type="paragraphXXXL" mr="quark">
            {getFormattedPoints(points || 0)}
          </Text>
          <Text type="paragraphMD">({formattedPrice})</Text>
        </Box>

        <InputBox
          label="Valor em pontos"
          placeholder="0"
          showLabel
          keyboardType="number-pad"
          value={pointsValue}
          onPressIn={() => handleSelectEvent('campo_pontos')}
          onChangeText={handleChangePoints}
          error={pointsError}
          textError="Você precisa informar um valor menor."
          textRight="Usar todos"
          onPressTextRight={handleUseAll}
        />

        <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
          <Box height="1px" bgColor="INTENSE_COLOR_02" width="45%" />
          <Icon
            name="icon-switch-vertical"
            size="xxs"
            color="INTENSE_COLOR_03"
            mr="nano"
            ml="nano"
          />
          <Box height="1px" bgColor="INTENSE_COLOR_02" width="45%" />
        </Box>

        <InputBox
          label="Valor em reais"
          placeholder="R$0,00"
          showLabel
          keyboardType="decimal-pad"
          onPressIn={() => handleSelectEvent('campo_valor')}
          value={reaisValue}
          onChangeText={handleChangeReais}
          error={reaisError}
          textError="Você precisa informar um valor menor."
        />
        {isLoadingParameterization && (
          <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
            <Skeleton width={width - 40} height={60} />
          </Box>
        )}
        {!isLoadingParameterization && (
          <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
            <Text type="paragraphMD">
              Cada {pointsQuantity || '0'} pontos valem {convertionValue || formatCurrencyBR(0)} de
              cashback na fatura.
            </Text>
          </Box>
        )}
      </Box>
      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
        <Button
          disabled={
            pointsValue === '' ||
            reaisValue === '' ||
            pointsValue === '0' ||
            reaisValue === '0' ||
            pointsError ||
            reaisError
          }
          text="Continuar"
          mt="large"
          mb="small"
          onPress={() => {
            const ptsSel = getParsePoints(pointsValue);
            const reaisSel = getParsedCurrency(reaisValue);
            handleContinue({ pointsSelected: ptsSel, reaisSelected: reaisSel });
          }}
          testID="invoice-cashback-button"
        />
      </Box>
      <BackDropBottom modalHeight={height * 0.53} modalizeRef={modalizeRef1}>
        <Text type="subtitleXXL" mb="smaller" mt="smaller" align="center">
          Use seus pontos para pagar menos na fatura
        </Text>

        <ListTextIcon items={items} ml="nano" mr="nano" type="list" mb="smaller" />

        <Button text="Entendi" onPress={handleModalizeContinue} />
      </BackDropBottom>
    </BasicScreen>
  );
};

export default InvoiceCashbackPointsView;

## INVOICE CASHBACK POINTS MIGRATION CODE REVIEW

**Final Verdict: FAIL - NOT PRODUCTION READY**

The migration has 8 TypeScript type errors and several structural issues that need resolution before deployment. While the Redux removal is complete and the Zustand integration is correctly implemented, there are critical type mismatches and interface inconsistencies.

---

### CHECKLIST RESULTS

✅ **1. No Redux imports remain in the screen**
- **PASS**: No redux, Redux, mapStateToProps, mapDispatchToProps, or connect() imports found
- Only Zustand hook (usePoints) is used

✅ **2. usePoints hook is properly destructured**
- **PASS**: Hook is correctly destructured at line 21-26:
  ```typescript
  const {
    points,
    pointsQuantity,
    isLoadingParameterization,
    requestParameterization,
  } = usePoints();
  ```

✅ **3. No inline Redux selectors**
- **PASS**: Component uses hook-based selectors only

✅ **4. No mapStateToProps/mapDispatchToProps functions**
- **PASS**: None found

✅ **5. No connect() HOC wrapper**
- **PASS**: Component directly exported as default

✅ **6. Props type doesn't depend on Redux**
- **PASS**: Props inherit from NativeStackScreenProps (line 34-35 in models)

✅ **7. Component is directly exported**
- **PASS**: Line 272: `export default InvoiceCashbackPoints;`

⚠️ **8. Follows project style guide (Hook Component pattern)**
- **PARTIAL PASS**: Structure follows the pattern (logic in index.tsx, render in view.tsx), but some implementation deviates from clean patterns

✅ **9. Clean code - no dead imports or unused variables**
- **PASS**: No unused imports, dead code, or orphaned variables

✅ **No console.log statements**
- **PASS**: Clean of debug logging

✅ **No TODO/FIXME comments**
- **PASS**: No technical debt markers found

---

### CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND

#### **1. TypeScript Type Errors (8 total) - BLOCKING**

**Location: `/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx`**

**Error 1 & 2 - Ref Type Mismatch (Lines 210, 229)**
```
Property 'open' does not exist on type 'never'
Property 'close' does not exist on type 'never'
```
**Root Cause**: `useRef(null)` creates `React.MutableRefObject<null>`, not properly typed for Modalize ref
```typescript
const modalizeRef1 = useRef(null);  // ❌ Missing PropsModalizeRef type
modalizeRef1.current?.open();       // ❌ Type error: 'never' has no property 'open'
```
**Fix Required**:
```typescript
const modalizeRef1 = useRef<PropsModalizeRef>(null);
```

**Error 3 - Function Return Type Mismatch (Line 245)**
```
Type '() => any[]' is not assignable to type 'CashbackInvoiceItem[]'
```
**Root Cause**: Passing function reference instead of array
```typescript
cashbackInvoiceList={invoiceList}  // ❌ invoiceList is a function, not an array
```
**Fix Required**: Call the function or rename the property

**Error 4 - Unused Props in Type Definition (Line 260)**
```
Type '{ icon: any; iconColor: any; ... }' is not assignable
```
**Root Cause**: Mismatch between what's passed to view and what view expects

**Location: `/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx`**

**Errors 5-8 - Box Props Type Errors (Lines 67, 91, 115, 120)**
```
Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'boolean | undefined'
```
**Root Cause**: Design system Box component expects boolean for direction, receiving string
```typescript
<Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">  // ❌ "row" should be boolean or enum
```
**Issue**: Likely design system version mismatch or incorrect property usage

**Error 9 - Function Signature Mismatch (View Line 144)**
```
Expected 0 arguments, but got 1
```
**Root Cause**: view.tsx calls `handleContinue({ pointsSelected, reaisSelected })` but types define `handleContinue: () => void`
```typescript
// In models:
handleContinue: () => void;  // ❌ No parameters

// In view (line 144):
handleContinue({ pointsSelected: ptsSel, reaisSelected: reaisSel });  // ❌ Passing object
```

---

### STRUCTURAL ISSUES

#### **Issue 1: Unused Type Props**
Files: `/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/models/index.ts` (Lines 10, 12)

Props defined but not used:
- `cashbackInvoiceList?: CashbackInvoiceItem[]` - defined but never destructured in view
- `price?: number` - passed but never used

These are dead type definitions creating confusion.

#### **Issue 2: Handler Signature Inconsistency**
The `handleContinue` callback has:
- **Type definition**: `handleContinue: () => void` (no parameters)
- **Index implementation**: Takes no parameters, handles logic internally
- **View usage**: Calls with parameters `{ pointsSelected, reaisSelected }`

This is a fundamental contract violation between container and presentational layers.

#### **Issue 3: Complex Props Passing Pattern**
Line 245: `cashbackInvoiceList={invoiceList}` - passing function instead of computed value
This suggests the helper function should be memoized or called before passing:
```typescript
// Current (❌):
const invoiceList = (): any[] => { ... };
cashbackInvoiceList={invoiceList}

// Should be (✅):
const invoiceList = useMemo(() => { ... }, [dependencies]);
```

---

### CODE QUALITY OBSERVATIONS

**Positive Aspects:**
- Clean separation of logic (index) and presentation (view)
- Proper use of Zustand hook with `useShallow` for performance
- No Redux pollution
- Analytics events properly integrated
- Error handling for parameterization loading

**Concerns:**
- Heavy use of local state (pointsValue, reaisValue, errors) in container - could be extracted
- No loading state for initial data request (line 30-32)
- Magic strings for event tracking (should use constants)
- Modalize ref pattern could be abstracted to custom hook

---

### SUMMARY

| Category | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Redux Removal | ✅ Complete | No Redux imports remain |
| Zustand Integration | ✅ Correct | usePoints hook properly used |
| TypeScript Safety | ❌ **8 Errors** | MUST FIX before merge |
| Code Organization | ✅ Good | Hook Component pattern followed |
| Code Cleanliness | ✅ Clean | No debug code or dead imports |
| Type Consistency | ❌ **Breaking** | Handler signature mismatches |

**Blocker Issues:**
1. 8 TypeScript type errors that prevent compilation
2. Function signature mismatch between container and view (handleContinue)
3. Ref typing issues preventing modal operations
4. Unused props in type definitions creating confusion

1. Fix useRef type annotation for Modalize (PropsModalizeRef)
2. Resolve handleContinue function signature - either remove parameters from view or add to type definition
3. Remove unused props (cashbackInvoiceList, price) from models
4. Fix Box direction prop (check design system usage)
5. Run `npm run typecheck` and verify zero errors
6. Test modal open/close functionality on device

**Estimated Fix Time:** 15-20 minutes
